findings.message: ItemRead exposes owner_email (PII) in the external API response. This field is serialized back to clients on both GET /items and POST /items. The Item model comment explicitly states owner_email must not be included in external responses, so echoing it here leaks PII to API consumers.
findings.suggestion: Remove owner_email from the ItemRead response schema so the email is never serialized to clients. If a contact display is required, expose a masked/redacted value or a separate authorized endpoint instead.

findings.message: ItemCreate collects owner_email (PII) from request bodies, but create_item ignores it (only name is persisted). Collecting PII that serves no stated purpose and is not used violates data-minimization; it also sets up the response schema to echo client-supplied PII.
findings.suggestion: Drop owner_email from ItemCreate unless it is actually needed and persisted for a documented purpose; if it is needed, ensure it is handled and stored per the model's PII guidance rather than echoed back.
